_comment: 2D sweep: rocking frequency (omega_b) × fill level.
Scientific basis
The first sloshing natural frequency f1 = (1/2π)√(g·k1·tanh(k1·h_b))
with k1=π/Lx, h_b=fill·Ly shifts with fill level.  The paper (Kim et al.
2025) identifies a 3:1 superharmonic resonance at f_b≈22.5 rpm for fill=0.5.
This sweep maps how that resonance locus moves across fill levels:
fill=0.30 → resonance near f_b=17.5 rpm  (f1≈54.2 rpm)
fill=0.40 → resonance near f_b=20.0 rpm  (f1≈62.0 rpm)
fill=0.50 → resonance near f_b=22.5 rpm  (f1≈68.6 rpm, paper-confirmed)
fill=0.60 → resonance near f_b=25.0 rpm  (f1≈74.1 rpm)
fill=0.70 → resonance near f_b=27.5 rpm  (f1≈78.9 rpm)
Parameter limits
omega_b: paper's 10-point frequency grid [15–37.5 rpm], converted to rad/s.
Lower bound 15 rpm (1.5708 rad/s): paper's lower limit; below this the
steady streaming is very weak and simulation time becomes impractical.
Upper bound 37.5 rpm (3.9270 rad/s): paper's laminar–turbulent transition.
fill_level: [0.30, 0.70] matching config/param_space.yaml bounds.
Lower bound 0.30: shallow-water regime begins; below this, wave breaking
near the walls is likely and the current code lacks a contact-angle model.
Upper bound 0.70: above this the air headspace (~0.021 m) becomes so thin
that gas-phase resistance dominates kLa rather than liquid-side transport.
theta_max: fixed at 7.0° (paper's primary sweep angle — strongest effects).
geometry: paper's 2L cellbag rectangle (a=Lx=0.25m, b=Ly=0.071m, n=8≈rect).
Expansion: cartesian product (10 × 5 = 50 runs).
Checkpoint groups: 5 groups × 10 segments, grouped by fill_level.
Runs sharing the same fill_level form one chain (f_b steps from low→high).
Different fill levels are physically incompatible for checkpoint restart
(the VOF interface height is encoded in the checkpoint field).
